stories.title: Zelenskyy to skip Ukraine recovery conference amid spat with Poland
stories.summary: Ukrainian Prime Minister Yulia Svyrydenko on Tuesday announced she will lead Kyiv’s delegation to this week’s Ukraine Recovery Conference in Gdańsk, with President Volodymyr Zelenskyy set to skip the event amid a growing diplomatic dispute with his Polish counterpart, Karol Nawrocki. “I am leading Ukraine’s delegation and our overall work at the Ukraine Recovery Conference 2026 in Gdańsk,” wrote Svyrydenko on X . The Ukrainian team will include business leaders, officials, lawmakers and lo

stories.title: Czech president sues prime minister over NATO attendance
stories.summary: Czech President Petr Pavel on Tuesday said that he had filed a lawsuit against the government, challenging Prime Minister Andrej Babiš’s decision to not include him in the government delegation attending the NATO summit in Ankara in July. The competency suit, which was lodged with Czechia’s Constitutional Court, asks for clarity on who has the authority to decide whether the head of state may attend the NATO summit. The court is due to take up the matter during its plenary session on

stories.title: Assault on facts and truth led to Brexit | Letters
stories.summary: Readers reflect on the 2016 referendum and its lasting impact Winston Churchill once said : “No one pretends that democracy is perfect or all-wise. Indeed it has been said that democracy is the worst form of government except for all those other forms that have been tried from time to time.” The Brexit campaign was run by multimillionaires who loathed regulation, and they persuaded people who appeared to have not been educated about politics or economics at school, or received alternative views

stories.title: Volodymyr Zelenskyy to skip postwar conference amid tensions with Poland
stories.summary: Ukraine’s president will not attend after sparking Polish ‘outrage’ over naming of military unit Volodymyr Zelenskyy will skip a high-level conference on the postwar reconstruction of Ukraine amid a deepening rift with Poland over his naming of a military unit for one that killed tens of thousands of Poles during the second world war. Ukraine’s president had been expected to co-host the Ukraine Recovery Conference, which begins in the Polish coastal city of Gdańsk on Thursday, but the Ukrainian

stories.title: Hungary hits pause on EU membership bids of Ukraine, Moldova
stories.summary: BRUSSELS — Hungary has delayed a key procedural step needed to push forward the EU membership bids of Ukraine and Moldova, according to two EU diplomats. Kyiv and Chisinau reached a milestone on their path into the bloc when EU countries unanimously approved, on June 15, the opening of the first formal negotiating chapter for both countries. The move had been blocked for years by former Hungarian Prime Minister Viktor Orbán, who opposed Ukraine’s EU membership. (Ukraine and Moldova&#8

stories.title: Canadian police warn of possible copycat attacks after deadly shootout in Montreal
stories.summary: Assailant behind shooting that left three people dead wrote ‘incel’-like manifesto that was posted by a far-right outlet Police in Canada are warning of possible copycat attacks after three people died in a shootout in Montreal and the assailant’s lengthy manifesto, which called for “a new bloodletting”, was posted online by a far-right outlet. The document contains many of the hallmark grievances of the “involuntary celibacy” – or “incel” – movement in addition racist and misogynistic conspirac

stories.title: Top auto regulator investigates deadly Tesla crash into Texas home
stories.summary: The vehicle was using an automated driving feature at high speed and killed a 76-year-old woman standing in the home Sign up for the Breaking News US newsletter email The top US auto regulator opened an investigation on Monday after a Tesla using an automated driving feature slammed into a Texas home at high speed and killed a 76-year-old woman standing inside. The National Highway Traffic Safety Administration (NHTSA) said it’s opening a special investigation into the Tesla Model 3 crash on Fri

stories.title: Whistleblower investigating Ecuadorian president’s family business was murdered, activists say
stories.summary: Monika Silva Koniuszek died from a blow to the head and strangulation, a postmortem found, despite government claim of suicide Campaigners in Ecuador say a Polish anti-corruption activist who investigated allegations against the family business of the country’s rightwing president was murdered to silence her. Monika Silva Koniuszek, 41, was found dead in her home in Montañita, a coastal town in Ecuador’s Santa Elena province. The single mother of daughters aged four and nine, was found on the fl

stories.title: Poland, Ukraine: Dispute between two close allies deepens
stories.summary: Polish President Karol Nawrocki last week stripped Ukrainian President Volodymyr Zelenskyy of Poland's highest state honor. How did it come to this? And what has it got to do with next year's election in Poland?

stories.title: The remarkable story of Péter Szijjártó’s best friend and the billions of public funds
stories.summary: According to a Direkt36 investigation, the company owned by Péter Szijjártó’s best friend, Szilárd Benkő, was involved in an African investment project financed by a Hungarian loan and worked on numerous other projects related to foreign affairs. The former minister claims he had no idea about these deals.

stories.title: Zelenszkij nem megy el az Ukrajna újjáépítéséről szóló lengyelországi konferenciára
stories.summary: Mélyponton van a lengyel–ukrán-viszony.

stories.title: How 100 hospitals switched to pen and paper to defeat a national cyber-attack
stories.summary: For four days, dozens of Romanian hospitals went offline, as cyber-experts sought to defeat the hackers.

stories.title: Hungarian Parliament adopts media law, effectively abolishing current public media
stories.summary: Restoring the independence of the public media service was one of the Tisza Party's election promises. The ministerial commissioner responsible for the area noted that this is the first step, with the next one set to follow in the fall.

stories.summary: Nem a pénzhiány a legnagyobb probléma, de akkor mégis hogyan lehetne előrelépni? Két olyan szemléletváltó dologgal, ami Nyugaton már működik.

stories.title: Orbán kormányzásának végére kiüresedett a V4, Magyarnak most nagy tervei vannak vele
stories.summary: A visegrádi négyek kormányfőinek gödöllői csúcsát hetek óta optimista nyilatkozatok előzték meg, a tetszhalott V4-et mindenki újra akarja építeni. A csúcson azonban előkerülhetnek ideológiai különbségek és a Beneš-dekrétumok körüli vita is árnyékot vet a teljes újraindításra.

stories.summary: A dél-koreai Hanon Systems Hungary Kft. székesfehérvári és pécsi egységét érinti a munkabeszüntetés.

stories.title: Muri Enikő is visszafizette az NKA-tól kapott milliókat
stories.summary: A Harcosok Klubja rendezvényen Orbánnal Himnuszt éneklő énekesnő is beállt Kis Grófo és több más nyertes mellé, így már az NKA-s keret tizedét visszafizették.

stories.title: 130 millió forintot követel a Magyar Honvédségtől a vértesszőlősi siklóernyő-baleset áldozatának családja
stories.summary: A siklóernyős sértetlenül landolt egy fán, a segítségére siető honvédségi helikopter rotorszele miatt zuhant 20 métert. Egy hónappal később belehalt a sérüléseibe. A Magyar Honvédség szerint a férfi nagy mértékben hozzájárult ahhoz, hogy a baleset végül tragédiába fulladt, és vitatja, hogy kártérítést kellene fizetnie.

stories.summary: Ezzel tizennyolcra nőtt a Magyar Honvédség Gripenjeinek száma.

stories.title: "They don't challenge my political statements, but my body" – study on gender-based political violence paints grim picture
stories.summary: Almost every Hungarian female lawmaker has a story linked to sexism and has experienced online, sexual or psychological abuse, but none of them have reported it. A study covering five European countries found that Hungary’s poor results cannot be separated from how the Orbán regime operated.

stories.summary: A portugálok első vb-meccséhez nem sokat tett hozzá, Üzbegisztán ellen egy félidő alatt berámolt két gólt.

stories.summary: Az óriásplakátokat betiltaná, a többi reklámfelületet nagyon erősen korlátozná a Főpolgármesteri Hivatal rendelettervezete.

stories.summary: Napenergiában EU-elsők vagyunk, a legnagyobb kihívás viszont az épületek szigetelése. A Magyarország jövő időben új része az energiaellátás fejlesztéséről szól.

stories.title: The Guardian view on the death of Carlo Ginzburg: a historian who taught us to think about outsiders | Editorial
stories.summary: The work of one of Italy’s greatest scholars focused on ordinary lives oppressed by power and prejudice. That approach resonates today Reflecting on the genesis of his most famous work, Carlo Ginzburg wrote that by immersing himself in the trial of a 16th-century miller burned by the Roman Inquisition, he turned a possible footnote into a book. stories.title: Romania's Dan taps new PM, breaks democratic norms
stories.summary: President Nicusor Dan has nominated a new prime minister, without consulting Romania’s governing parties. Critics accuse Dan of breaking democratic rules.

stories.title: Zelenskyy returns Poland's highest honor as row deepens
stories.summary: Ukraine's president has returned a medal to Poland after a decision to strip him of the country's highest honor. Warsaw has been one of Kyiv's key allies since Russia began its war in Ukraine.

stories.title: UK prioritised ties with UAE over averting mass atrocities in Sudan, MPs to be told
stories.summary: Foreign Office failed to act on warnings of genocide due to ‘pressure’ from emirates, Yale human rights investigator will tell a parliamentary select committee The British government had received intelligence that Ethiopia appeared to be supporting a genocidal militia in Sudan’s civil war as far back as 2024 but did not go public with the news for fear of upsetting the United Arab Emirates (UAE), a parliamentary committee will hear. stories.title: Ukraine says major Crimea bridge destroyed in latest attack
stories.summary: Ukraine continues to hit infrastructure on the Crimea to isolate the Russian-annexed peninsula. Meanwhile, Russia attacked Volodymyr Zelenskyy's hometown with a ballistic missile in a deadly strike.

stories.title: Kenyan minister orders halt to construction of US Ebola facility
stories.summary: Decision comes after Aden Duale was held in contempt for ignoring previous high court ruling to stop work Kenya’s health minister told a court he had ordered preparations for a US-run Ebola quarantine facility to stop, after being held in contempt for ignoring a previous order to end work. stories.title: Turkey arrests more than 200 in crackdown before NATO summit
stories.summary: The Ankara prosecutor's office said 209 people were detained, while 32 others remained at large. Turkey has announced a ban on demonstrations amid preparations for next month's NATO summit in the capital.

stories.title: Irish government to contribute £197m to cross-border rail services
stories.summary: The money will come from the last allocation from the Irish government's Shared Island Fund.

stories.title: Starmer government pauses new ‘major’ policy announcements
stories.summary: LONDON — The U.K. government will not announce “new major policy or spending commitments” until Keir Starmer departs No. 10, according to a spokesperson for the outgoing prime minister. Starmer set out a timetable for his departure on Monday , announcing that Labour will begin a leadership contest in July to identify a successor. If there is a full contest, it would run until September — but new Makerfield MP Andy Burnham is overwhelmingly expected to run unopposed, meaning he would succeed Star

stories.title: Réguler les géants de la tech, un casse-tête pour la présidence irlandaise de l’UE
stories.summary: Alors que l’Irlande se prépare à bâtir des compromis entre les gouvernements européens sur des dossiers épineux concernant la tech ces six prochains mois, un énorme éléphant sera dans la pièce : les relations étroites que le pays entretient avec les géants américains du secteur. Car l’île d’Emeraude accueille 16 des 20 plus grandes entreprises technologiques mondiales, et le secteur y emploie plus de 100 000 personnes. stories.title: Fact-checking Brexit's key promises 10 years on – video

From immigration, a bus promising £350m a week for the NHS, to bendy bananas, the 'most consequential decision in modern history' had mixed results From bendy bananas to £350m for the NHS – how many Brexit promises actually came true? stories.title: Israel continues to commit genocide by targeting children in Gaza, UN inquiry finds

stories.title: Department of Homeland Security will allow Iran to travel to US early for World Cup match

The team had planned to lodge an official complaint with Fifa about the “restrictions imposed by the organisers” at the World Cup . Iran have been training in Mexico and were only allowed to enter the United States 24 hours before their first two ma

stories.title: Trump says DC reflecting pool repairs may not be done in time for 4 July
stories.summary: US president planned to revamp the now algae-stricken pool ahead of the holiday and America’s 250th birthday Donald Trump has acknowledged that repairs to the algae-stricken reflecting pool in Washington DC may not be completed in time for the Fourth of July, when the president plans to convene a big celebration on the National Mall to ring in the country’s 250th birthday . The iconic body of water began filling with algae blooms and peeling paint after the Trump administration ordered a $14.2m

stories.title: Donald Trump to present World Cup trophy to winners, says Gianni Infantino
stories.summary: Two men due to share trophy-presenting on 19 July ‘We are together all the time’, says Fifa president Donald Trump will hand over the World Cup trophy to the winners at the final on 19 July, Gianni Infantino has said. Infantino and Trump have forged a close relationship in the buildup to these finals, but the US president has made very few public pronouncements concerning the tournament since it began on 11 June. Continue reading...

stories.title: Bev Craig to stand as Labour candidate for Greater Manchester mayor
stories.summary: Leader of city council braces for bitter fight with Reform UK in byelection for Andy Burnham’s replacement Labour’s candidate to replace Andy Burnham as Greater Manchester mayor has been named as Bev Craig, the leader of the city council. Burnham, who could be prime minister in under four weeks, is expected to campaign heavily for Labour in a tight contest with Reform UK on 30 July. Continue reading...

During a red alert – the highest level – the ministry advises people to ea

stories.title: Burnham and Starmer hold ‘frosty’ meeting to thrash out transition of power
stories.summary: With Burnham and his team potentially having only weeks before he becomes PM, Starmer has agreed to give him access to civil service Keir Starmer has met Andy Burnham for the first time since the Makerfield byelection in what sources said was a “frosty” meeting to thrash out a transition of power. The prime minister has agreed for his likely successor to have talks with the civil service to smooth his path, but there is deep resentment within his inner circle towards Burnham for ousting Starmer.

stories.title: Rubio insists strait of Hormuz will be toll-free as he arrives for Gulf meeting
stories.summary: US secretary of state seeks to reassure UAE, Kuwait and Bahrain over security and US-Iran ceasefire deal Middle East crisis live – latest updates The US secretary of state, Marco Rubio, has said no country, including Iran, would be allowed to charge tolls for shipping in the strait of Hormuz as he sought to reassure US allies in the Gulf that Washington would take a firm line in peace negotiations with Tehran. Rubio is to meet Gulf allies on Tuesday and Wednesday in an attempt to reassure them t

stories.title: Europe’s heatwave drives electricity prices to new highs as demand soars
stories.summary: Great Britain has paid at least six times the normal price for imported power as millions turn on air conditioning and windfarm output sags The heatwave has prompted a sharp rise in electricity prices across European markets as millions turn to air conditioners and electric fans to battle record high temperatures, which have also caused a string of power plant outages across the continent. Great Britain imported electricity from Europe at more than six times the normal price on Tuesday as the hi

stories.title: Three in five gen Z Britons would like new vote to rejoin EU, poll finds
stories.summary: Exclusive: Data reveals 60% of 18 to 28-year-olds would vote to rejoin bloc if given the opportunity A generation of young Britons who were locked out of the 2016 EU referendum because of their age now believe that Brexit has failed, with a majority demanding a fresh vote to rejoin the EU, exclusive polling shows. Gen Z Britons show deep dissatisfaction with the UK’s departure from the EU, according to new polling of 18- to 28-year-olds conducted by the thinktank More in Common and shared with t

stories.title: Germany's pension plans draw praise and outrage
stories.summary: A 33-point plan to reform Germany's pension system has drawn praise and criticism in almost equal measure. Centrist politicians have called it a good compromise, but opposition parties on the left and right are unhappy.

stories.title: More than a unibrow: The enduring appeal of Frida Kahlo
stories.summary: She shunned feminine beauty ideals, was bisexual and a political radical, all while being brutally honest in her art. A major Frida Kahlo exhibition opens at London's Tate Modern.

stories.title: Germany news: Merz hails pension reform proposals
stories.summary: Chancellor Merz says a proposed pension reform package should be fully implemented. A survey has shown that almost half of teachers in Germany find pupils' behavior stressful. DW has more.

stories.title: EU hosts Taliban for migrant return talks in Brussels
stories.summary: The EU is hosting Taliban officials after Belgium granted temporary visas for talks on returning failed Afghan asylum-seekers. The meeting is one of the bloc's highest-level contacts with the Taliban since 2021.

stories.title: Europe is spending billions to rearm, so why the delays?
stories.summary: European countries have been trying to scale up their military capabilities, but their current defense production and procurement model is not fit for purpose, say experts.

stories.summary: Monuments in Paris are working on reduced hours, and dozens of people in France have drowned during the heat wave. Britain, France, Italy and Spain have issued red alerts and health warnings. More on DW.

stories.title: Wanted: Jobs, critical minerals as Zambia's election nears

stories.title: Afghan Taliban clamp down on women, smartphones, protests
stories.summary: The Taliban are tightening the screws on women's clothing, shooting at protesters and ordering even their own officials to stop using smartphones. Behind the regime's omnipresent control, there are signs of deep anxiety.

stories.title: Experts fired by Trump resurrect mothballed climate website
stories.summary: Fired US federal workers have revived a defunct climate website — pushing back as the Trump administration escalates cuts to publicly funded science and research.

stories.title: Hong Kong pro-democracy publisher Jimmy Lai honored by DW
stories.summary: Before being imprisoned for 20 years, Jimmy Lai founded Hong Kong's largest pro-democracy newspaper. His son said the award shows that those who fight for the freedom of others "are never alone."

stories.title: Colombia: The 'Tiger's' victory signals a rightward shift
stories.summary: With the election of Abelardo de la Espriella, an ultra-right-wing outsider has won the presidential runoff in Colombia for the first time. The tight victory marks a political shift and could trigger new conflicts.

stories.title: US, Iran give conflicting claims over Hormuz fees
stories.summary: US top diplomat Rubio said Washington will not accept tolls on shipping in the Strait of Hormuz. But Iran has said they want to charge "maritime service fees." And Israel and Lebanon are set for talks in the US.

stories.title: Scotland: Sturgeon's ex-husband Peter Murrell jailed for 5 years and 3 months
stories.summary: Peter Murrell pleaded guilty to embezzling funds in his role as chief executive of the Scottish National Party. He held the SNP's purse strings while his then-wife, Nicola Sturgeon, was Scottish First Minister.

stories.title: Lebanon's lose-lose: Is there another way out of conflict?
stories.summary: The new US-Iran peace deal seems to be forcing Lebanon to choose between Israel's sphere of influence or Iran's. It wants neither. But could there be a better alternative, achieved with the help of regional diplomacy?

stories.title: Ebola in DR Congo sees record first-month caseload
stories.summary: The DR Congo has recorded the highest confirmed number of cases in the first month of any Ebola outbreak in Africa. Meanwhile, Kenya's health minister says he is halting a US-backed Ebola quarantine center.

stories.title: Dettol apologises after ad to clean up 'toxic men' backfires in China
stories.summary: The ad sparked accusations of sexism in China, after featuring a man looking for a partner who is "not tainted by other men".

stories.title: Montreal shooting leaves officer, civilian and suspect dead
stories.summary: It was the first time in 24 years that a Montreal police officer was killed in the line of duty, the city's police chief said.

stories.title: Spider which uses spring trap to capture prey discovered in Australia
stories.summary: Researchers say their hunting method - which allows them to prey on dangerous ants - is unprecedented.

stories.title: Trump anticipates better relationship with Colombia under new leader
stories.summary: Abelardo de la Espriella, who preliminary results suggest is Colombia's next president, had Trump's endorsement.

stories.title: Iran says no new commitments on nuclear sites after Vance says inspectors to be invited back

stories.title: From cool-down spots to chalk on windows - how Europe is coping with the heat

stories.title: Lost memoir of Hiroshima survivor found after decades in US archive

The 230-page memoir was written almost 80 years ago by Kiyoshi Tanimoto, who witnessed the city’s destruction after the atomic bomb was dropped in 1945. He will now be portrayed in a feature film by Takehiro Hira

stories.title: ‘I remember the shock’, ‘It can still be reversed’ – what do Europeans think of Brexit now?

stories.summary: How five months in 2016 that encompassed Boris Johnson siding with Vote Leave, Jo Cox’s murder and David Cameron’s resignation shaped the UK’s future David Cameron, having promised in 2013 that a future Conservative government would offer a referendum on Britain’s membership of the EU , announces the date of the vote: 23 June 2016. The next day, Boris Johnson, then the mayor of London , says he will campaign for leave . Continue reading...

stories.title: Bangladesben úgy ünneplik az argentin válogatott győzelmeit, mint ha ők nyertek volna. De miért?

stories.summary: A II. kerületi alpolgármester szerint főnöke továbbra is felelős az önkormányzat döntéseiért.

stories.summary: It had planned to abandon the fuel, but the higher cost of natural gas may make it think again.

stories.title: Russian troop build-up threatens city seen as key to seizing Ukraine's Donbas
stories.summary: If Kostyantynivka falls, Russian forces would be able push towards Ukraine's last remaining strongholds in the east.

stories.title: Trump says repairs to algae-plagued Reflecting Pool will begin immediately
stories.summary: Despite a multi-million dollar renovation, the Washington DC landmark has peeling paint and algae. Trump claims vandals are partly to blame.

stories.title: Largest ever cocaine bust in Australia after police raid underground bunker
stories.summary: Police seized 2.7 tonnes of cocaine worth an estimated A$816m after searching a property in western Sydney.

stories.title: Trump-backed political outsider wins Colombia election, initial count shows
stories.summary: Abelardo de la Espriella appears to have narrowly defeated his rival Iván Cepeda, who says the preliminary count is "not yet official or binding".

stories.title: Red heat alerts issued in France, Italy and Spain as 40C temperatures forecast
stories.summary: The heatwave conditions are forecast to intensify in the coming days across central and western Europe.

stories.title: Sweat, tears and camaraderie as 20,000 runners take on world’s largest ultramarathon
stories.summary: For one day every June, South Africa’s searing racial inequality seems to melt away at Comrades race In the early morning dark, thousands of runners waited, jostling with anticipation. South Africa’s national anthem rang out. Then the haunting swell of Shosholoza , first sung by Zimbabwean migrant workers in South Africa’s goldmines. Finally, that unmistakable, spine-tingling piano: Chariots of Fire. Runners gather before the start of the marathon Continue reading...

stories.title: Dutch PM apologises for Moluccan soldiers’ mistreatment after Indonesian independence
stories.summary: Rob Jetten acknowledges grief and pain of Moluccan families as crowdfunded monument unveiled in Rotterdam The Dutch prime minister, Rob Jetten, has formally apologised for the “heartless” mistreatment of thousands of Moluccan soldiers who fought for the Dutch colonial army during Indonesia’s struggle for independence. About 12,500 people – men who had served in the Royal Dutch East Indies and their families – came from a group of Indonesian islands to the Netherlands in 1951, many having been gi

stories.title: UK's Starmer mulls 'political realities' after Burnham by-election
stories.summary: A member of UK Prime Minister Keir Starmer's Cabinet said it would be "delusional" to pretend there was no threat to his position. But he also said he had no information suggesting he might quit, as a newspaper reported.

stories.title: Scottish police arrest man over Edinburgh attacks
stories.summary: Five people were injured in the attacks on Friday that were described by Prime Minister Keir Starmer as being motivated by "anti-Muslim hatred."

stories.title: Taliban order ban on smartphones as officials shown destroying devices
stories.summary: Directive aimed at government workers, but reports of wider implementation spark warnings of future Afghanistan-wide prohibition The Taliban have ordered a sweeping ban on the use of smartphones by government officials – in what some analysts say could foreshadow broader, population-level restrictions. In a directive issued by the Taliban’s military courts and reviewed by the Guardian, the ban was to take effect this week and prohibits “high rank, low rank, general mujahideen, or service staff”

stories.title: Undav heroics help Germany beat Ivory Coast
stories.summary: In Germany's second 2026 World Cup game, Deniz Undav helped Germany to a comeback win against Ivory Coast in Toronto.

stories.title: Germany could scrap law banning insults against politicians
stories.summary: Germany's special law prohibiting the insulting of politicians has led to cases that many people consider absurd. Some members of the government want to abolish the law; others think there's a good reason to retain it.

stories.title: Germany's media rocked by AI scandal
stories.summary: Two German outlets were forced to delete articles that used undisclosed AI. Many fear that reliance on the technology will damage the credibility of German media.

stories.title: Parisians cool off in canal amid 'red alert' heatwave
stories.summary: France has issued red heatwave alerts for around half the country as temperatures soar to record levels.

stories.title: Vietnam police rescue hundreds of cats stolen for meat by crime ring
stories.summary: Major operation launched after spate of pet thefts in Ho Chi Minh City, according to local media Police in Vietnam have rescued more than 400 cats in a bust of a cat meat crime ring in Ho Chi Minh City, according to animal welfare groups and local media reports. More than 40 cats were reunited with their owners after the multiday operation last week, but several dozen of those rescued have died due to the harsh conditions in which they were found, the groups said. Continue reading...

stories.title: ‘Period tax’ on sanitary products to be abolished, says Pakistan minister
stories.summary: Campaigners welcome announcement cutting levies on menstrual health items, but say their work to end period poverty is ‘far from over’ Pakistan plans to abolish “period tax”, in a victory for young campaigners who had taken the government to court over the charges. Finance minister Muhammad Aurangzeb announced that sanitary towels and related items were “daily necessities that are indispensable for women’s health, dignity and full participation in social activities”, and said he intended to remo

stories.title: Ghana conference calls for formal apology for transatlantic slave trade
stories.summary: Global framework for reparatory justice adopted at event includes demand for compensation and debt relief More than money: the logic of slavery reparations A global framework for reparatory justice has been adopted at a conference in Ghana, as African and Caribbean leaders demanded formal apologies from countries that benefited from the transatlantic slave trade. Heads of state and government and other officials formally approved the strategy on Friday at a gathering in a hotel in the capital, A

stories.title: Spain: PM's wife must face corruption trial, judge rules
stories.summary: A court in Madrid ruled that there is sufficient evidence against Begona Gomez, the wife of Prime Minister Pedro Sanchez. The 55-year-old university director is banned from leaving the country.

stories.title: Lululemon apologises after Japanese drum row at Great Wall yoga event
stories.summary: Online uproar follows Canadian brand’s use of taiko drum at sponsored festival held to celebrate Chinese culture The activewear brand Lululemon has apologised after a promotional event held on the Great Wall of China appeared to mistakenly feature a Japanese drum, prompting an uproar. The Canadian-headquartered company, known for its upmarket leggings, has been growing rapidly in China and arranged for a yoga festival to take place in late May on a section of the wall near Beijing. Continue read

stories.title: Weather tracker: Severe thunderstorms sweep Europe and east Asia
stories.summary: Strong winds and heavy rain batter Slovenia, while France experiences atypical heatwave Severe thunderstorms swept across the Balkans last week, bringing widespread destruction to parts of the region. The storms developed as unstable hot air lingered over the Adriatic Sea while a cold front plunged south-eastward. The front began its journey on 10 June in Slovenia, where the Slovenian Environment Agency recorded 65mph gusts at Ljubljana airport. Heavy rain also fell widely across the region with

stories.title: New exhibitions for Anish Kapoor: Art on the edge
stories.summary: His art challenges our perception: Forms vanish and perspectives shift. Anish Kapoor leads the viewer to the edge of the abyss — and sometimes, even beyond. Two new exhibitions revisit his work.

stories.title: Women tackling coal dust and other eco wins this week
stories.summary: Indian women fight back against toxic coal dust, the US hits a solar milestone, and there's good news for soil in Kenya and cars worldwide.

stories.title: Can Europe build its own AI heavyweights to rival the US?
stories.summary: With AI dominated by US firms, Europe is pushing for more independence. New partnerships, investments and startups aim to boost the continent's digital sovereignty, but challenges around funding and regulation remain.

stories.title: As heat waves grow more frequent and fierce, cities worldwide are racing to cool streets and reshape urban life. But can they adapt quickly enough?
stories.summary: As heat waves grow more frequent and fierce, cities worldwide are racing to cool streets and reshape urban life.

stories.title: What to expect from the strongest El Nino in over a century
stories.summary: Warnings of a mighty El Nino weather pattern this year could bring drought, flooding and extreme heat. Can we prepare for the worst of it?

stories.title: German journalist detained in Syria back home, lawyer says
stories.summary: Eva Maria Michelmann vanished in Syria in January as government forces advanced on Kurdish fighters in the northeast. The German government last week said it was working to get her home.

stories.title: EU ban on BPA chemical in food packaging takes effect
stories.summary: From July 2026 onwards, BPA will no longer be permitted in EU food packaging. The industrial chemical is known to interfere with the human hormonal system.

stories.title: Inequality is elementary to Germany's school system
stories.summary: Children's paths through Germany's school system are often determined before their first day of kindergarten, according to the national report on education. An elementary school in Bonn is offering a new model for kids.

stories.title: France bans alcohol during Fete de la Musique amid heat wave
stories.summary: France is taking additional measures as millions of people gear up for Fete de la Musique under punishing temperatures.

stories.title: Are Ukraine drones really exposing gaps in Russia's defense?
stories.summary: Once seen as almost impenetrable, Russian air defenses are now under growing strain from Ukrainian drones. A recent attack on Moscow has reignited debate over Russian vulnerabilities.

stories.title: Terror police probe suspected anti-Muslim attacks in Edinburgh
stories.summary: Counterterrorism police have taken charge after a series of violent attacks in the Scottish capital that left five men injured.

stories.title: Will Putin change tactics after Ukrainian drone attacks?
stories.summary: Steve Rosenberg explains what Russia's next steps may be after frequent Ukrainian drone attacks.

stories.title: Should the US impose a teen social media ban like the UK?
stories.summary: The UK is enforcing a social media ban for children under the age of 16. The BBC asked Americans of different ages if they thought it was a good idea.

stories.title: Would you choose to take a 22-hour non-stop flight?
stories.summary: The BBC asked Sydney locals if they would take the newly announced, longest ever commercial flight from Sydney to London.

stories.title: BBC sees destroyed villages in Israeli-occupied southern Lebanon
stories.summary: Travelling with a humanitarian convoy, BBC's Hugo Bachega has been given rare access to a part of Lebanon under Israeli occupation.

stories.title: Indian outrage over US killing of sailors mounts as leaders attend G7 summit
stories.summary: Relations at lowest ebb in years after Washington refuses to apologise for deaths in strait of Hormuz Fury has continued to mount in India over the US’s refusal to apologise for the deaths of Indian sailors killed in strikes in the strait of Hormuz, further straining relations between the two countries as their leaders meet at the G7 summit in France this week. Last week, three Indian seafarers, who were working on board commercial oil tankers, were killed when the US launched missile strikes on

stories.title: Sri Lanka sees ‘alarming’ rise in cybercrime as scam networks relocate from south-east Asia
stories.summary: Experts say criminal networks favour Sri Lanka due to ease of getting tourist visas and limited regulation on sim cards and internet connections Experts have warned that Sri Lanka is emerging as a hub for transnational cybercrime, after a crackdown in south-east Asia pushed Chinese-run criminal networks to relocate their vast scam operations. Sri Lankan police spokesperson Fredrick Wootler said the country was witnessing an “alarming increase of cybercrimes” perpetrated by people entering the co
